release_review.summary: Flower consumes published Floret v0.3.90 runtime contract hardening for projection recovery, independent token budgets, deletion fencing, and shared activity summary rebuilding without changing the Runtime Service compatibility window.
checked_surfaces: Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1; the compatibility epoch remains 5 and minimum Desktop/Runtime versions remain v0.7.0
Floret is consumed only from the published github.com/floegence/floret v0.3.90 module with GOWORK=off validation and no replace, go.work, package-manager link, or local sibling path wiring

Flower retries ErrTurnProjectionUnavailable through the public ReadTurnProjection API, validates ThreadID/TurnID/RunID, preserves streamed content on failure, and exposes only the Redeven product error floret_projection_unavailable
RunOptions.MaxInputTokens maps to cumulative Floret TurnLimits.MaxInputTokens while MaxOutputTokens remains a per-provider-request output ceiling; no derived total-token budget is introduced

Redeven threadstore transactions fence late run, event, tool-call, execution-span, continuation, todo, memory, and transcript writes after force deletion without changing Runtime Service request or response fields
Flower terminal lifecycle floors use published Floret RebuildActivitySummary instead of a Redeven-local summary reducer
Desktop attach/start, Local UI health, structured startup reports, sys.ping, Env App maintenance UI, gateway lifecycle, SSH bootstrap, and Runtime Service control methods remain unchanged
release_review.same_window_rationale: The compatibility window is unchanged because all behavior changes are internal to Flower's integration with the published Floret v0.3.90 module and Redeven's product threadstore. No Runtime Service wire request, response, method, startup report, attach/start handshake, sys.ping payload, Local UI health contract, Env App maintenance route, gateway lifecycle message, or SSH bootstrap contract changes, so existing v0.7.0 Desktop and Runtime binaries remain compatible.
